docs.title: Terracotta
docs.text:
Terracotta is a declarative project registry management tool for Minecraft developers. It manages Modrinth (and in the future Hangar/CurseForge) project metadata, description, tags, and versions using a simple YAML configuration file as the single source of truth.

  • Declarative Configuration

    Define your project info, description path, categories, tags, license, and version artifacts in terracotta.yaml.

    Getting started

  • Dry Runs (Plan)

    Preview all updates, tag changes, and version uploads before executing them, preventing incorrect uploads or metadata state drift.

    CLI commands

  • Maven SDK

    Integrate Terracotta's core directly into Gradle/Maven plugins, IDEs, or custom automation pipelines without the CLI.

    Core API

  • CI/CD Integration

    Easily integrate with GitHub Actions to apply registry changes automatically when you push tags or release artifacts.

    CI/CD Guide

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. You edit terracotta.yaml in your project root.
  2. Run terracotta plan to view a diff of local configuration vs remote Modrinth state.
  3. Run terracotta apply to push changes (metadata, upload versions, synchronize tags) to the remote registry.
~ Update description
~ Update tags (from: utility to: utility, paper)
+ Upload version 1.2.0

docs.title: Setup Requirements
docs.text: Component Version JVM / JDK 21+ Native Image GraalVM (for native executables) Target Registries Modrinth API (Hangar/CurseForge planned)

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub
  • Discord

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• [Core]: Canonical project and version domain models, provider abstractions (StateProvider, RegistryProvider), and DiffEngine calculating semantic operations. Includes support for full project creation when a project does not exist on the remote registry.
  • Why: Separates platform-agnostic business logic from registry integrations, facilitating robust diff calculations and enabling clean automation.
  • [CLI]: Command-line frontend supporting plan and apply command, complete with standard help options (--help) and colorized diff output.
  • Why: Provides developers with an ergonomic command-line interface to safely preview, get help on, and apply declarative updates.
  • [Modrinth]: Modrinth state and registry integration using Ktor Client and Kotlinx Serialization.
  • Why: Bootstraps the first concrete provider to sync project settings, metadata, and artifacts directly with Modrinth.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text: Terracotta follows clean code and modular design principles, isolating the core domain logic from the specific registry adapters (e.g. Modrinth) and the CLI interface.

docs.title: Layer & Module Overview
docs.text:
graph TD
    subgraph terracotta-cli["terracotta-cli (CLI App)"]
        App["App / Main"]
        Commands["PlanCommand / ApplyCommand"]
        ConfigParser["parseConfig"]
    end

    subgraph terracotta-provider-modrinth["terracotta-provider-modrinth (Adapter)"]
        ModrinthClient["ModrinthClient"]
        ModrinthState["ModrinthStateProvider"]
        ModrinthRegistry["ModrinthRegistryProvider"]
    end

    subgraph terracotta-core["terracotta-core (Core SDK)"]
        DiffEngine["DiffEngine"]
        StateProvider["StateProvider (Interface)"]
        RegistryProvider["RegistryProvider (Interface)"]
        Models["TerracottaProject / TerracottaVersion"]
        Operation["Operation (Sealed Interface)"]
    end

    App --> Commands
    Commands --> ConfigParser
    ConfigParser --> Models
    Commands --> DiffEngine
    Commands --> ModrinthState
    Commands --> ModrinthRegistry
    ModrinthState --> StateProvider
    ModrinthRegistry --> RegistryProvider
    ModrinthState --> ModrinthClient
    ModrinthRegistry --> ModrinthClient

docs.title: Core SDK (terracotta-core under modules/terracotta-core)
docs.text:
Package: io.github.beduality.terracotta.core.*

A pure platform-agnostic library containing canonical models, provider interfaces, and the semantic diff engine.

Class / Package Responsibility core.model.TerracottaProject Canonical model representing project config metadata, tags, license, and versions. core.model.TerracottaVersion Canonical model representing version metadata and the compiled artifact file path. core.provider.StateProvider Abstraction to fetch the remote project state. core.provider.RegistryProvider Abstraction to apply a list of generic operations to the remote registry. core.diff.DiffEngine Compares local and remote models to produce a set of semantic operations. core.diff.Operation Sealed interface representing mutations (UpdateMetadata, UpdateDescription, UpdateTags, UploadVersion).

docs.title: Modrinth Provider (terracotta-provider-modrinth under modules/terracotta-provider-modrinth)
docs.text:
Package: io.github.beduality.terracotta.provider.modrinth.*

Bridges the core interfaces with Modrinth's REST API using OkHttp and Jackson.

Class Responsibility ModrinthClient Low-level HTTP client executing requests against Modrinth. ModrinthStateProvider Fetches project info and versions and translates them into canonical models. ModrinthRegistryProvider Translates the computed operations list into concrete PATCH and POST API calls.

docs.title: CLI Module (terracotta-cli under modules/terracotta-cli)
docs.text:
Package: io.github.beduality.terracotta.cli.*

Command line interface providing plan and apply actions.

Class Responsibility App Picocli configuration commands and entry point. ConfigParser Parses the terracotta.yaml config and resolves file paths relative to the file.

docs.title: 1. Canonical Model
docs.text: Both the local configuration YAML file and the remote registry metadata are mapped to the same internal domain models: TerracottaProject and TerracottaVersion.

docs.title: 2. Diff Engine
docs.text: The DiffEngine computes a list of Operation objects to perform based on differences between local and remote models.

docs.title: 3. Providers
docs.text:
• StateProvider: Fetches remote registry assets and translates them into the Canonical Model.
  • RegistryProvider: Translates generic Operation lists into registry-specific actions (e.g. PATCH requests or file uploads).

docs.title: Request Flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    actor User
    participant CLI as App/ApplyCommand
    participant Parser as parseConfig
    participant State as ModrinthStateProvider
    participant Diff as DiffEngine
    participant Registry as ModrinthRegistryProvider

    User->>CLI: terracotta apply
    activate CLI
    CLI->>Parser: parseConfig(terracotta.yaml)
    Parser-->>CLI: Local Project Model
    CLI->>State: fetchProject(id)
    State-->>CLI: Remote Project Model
    CLI->>Diff: diff(local, remote)
    Diff-->>CLI: List of Operations
    CLI->>Registry: apply(id, operations)
    activate Registry
    Registry->>User: Update metadata / upload JARs
    deactivate Registry
    deactivate CLI

docs.title: Design Decisions
docs.text:
Why separate Core SDK from the CLI?

By keeping terracotta-core generic and publishing it to Maven Central, we enable developers to write custom Gradle, Maven, IDE, or CI/CD plugins directly on top of the domain layer, without forcing them to invoke command line subprocesses.

Why decouple registries using Provider Interfaces?

Abstracting operations behind the StateProvider and RegistryProvider interfaces ensures the core engine remains clean and free of network logic. Adding new providers in the future (e.g. Hangar or CurseForge) is as simple as implementing those interfaces.

docs.title: CI/CD Setup with GitHub Actions
docs.text: You can automate publishing version updates and syncing project metadata using GitHub Actions.

docs.title: 1. Store your Modrinth Token
docs.text: First, add your Modrinth API token to your GitHub repository secrets: 1. Go to your repository Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions. 2. Click New repository secret. 3. Name it MODRINTH_TOKEN and paste your API token.

docs.title: 2. Create the workflow file
docs.text:
Create a workflow file in .github/workflows/deploy.yml:

name: Deploy Plugin

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up JDK 21
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: 'zulu'
          java-version: '21'

      - name: Build Plugin Artifact
        run: ./gradlew build

      - name: Download & Install Terracotta CLI
        run: |
          # Download latest binary from GitHub Releases
          curl -sSL https://github.com/beduality/terracotta/releases/latest/download/terracotta-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/terracotta
          chmod +x /usr/local/bin/terracotta

      - name: Deploy with Terracotta
        env:
          MODRINTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.MODRINTH_TOKEN }}
        run: terracotta apply

Whenever you push a tag like v1.2.0, this workflow will build your plugin and execute Terracotta to upload your release artifact automatically.

docs.title: Implementing a Custom Provider
docs.text: Terracotta core is modular and does not depend on Modrinth. You can add support for other registries (e.g. CurseForge or Hangar) by implementing core interfaces in the SDK.

docs.title: 1. Implement StateProvider
docs.text:
The StateProvider interface is responsible for fetching the current project state from the remote registry:

package io.github.beduality.terracotta.core.provider

import io.github.beduality.terracotta.core.model.TerracottaProject

interface StateProvider {
    fun fetchProject(projectId: String): TerracottaProject?
}

docs.title: 2. Implement RegistryProvider
docs.text:
The RegistryProvider interface executes the operations emitted by the Diff Engine:

package io.github.beduality.terracotta.core.provider

import io.github.beduality.terracotta.core.diff.Operation

interface RegistryProvider {
    fun apply(projectId: String, operations: List<Operation>)
}

docs.title: 3. Run the Diff Engine
docs.text:
Use DiffEngine to compute differences between local configuration and the retrieved remote project state:

val localProject: TerracottaProject = ...
val remoteProject: TerracottaProject? = customStateProvider.fetchProject(localProject.id)

// Compute differences
val operations: List<Operation> = DiffEngine.diff(localProject, remoteProject)

// Apply changes
customRegistryProvider.apply(localProject.id, operations)

docs.title: Core API Reference
docs.text: The terracotta-core module is published to Maven Central, allowing developers to build IDE plugins, build-tool plugins (Gradle/Maven), or custom integration workflows.

docs.title: Maven Dependency
docs.text: implementation("io.github.beduality:terracotta-core:0.1.0")

docs.title: TerracottaProject
docs.text:
Holds project details and its versions:

data class TerracottaProject(
    val id: String,
    val name: String,
    val summary: String,
    val description: String,
    val versions: List<TerracottaVersion>,
    val tags: List<String>,
    val license: String
)

docs.title: TerracottaVersion
docs.text:
Holds version details:

data class TerracottaVersion(
    val version: String,
    val artifactPath: String,
    val gameVersions: List<String>
)

docs.title: Operation
docs.text:
A sealed interface representing mutations:

  • UpdateMetadata: Represents updates to name, summary, and license.
  • UpdateDescription: Represents updates to body/description.
  • UpdateTags: Represents category/tag updates.
  • UploadVersion: Represents uploading new version JARs.

docs.title: DiffEngine
docs.text:
Calculates changes required to transition from remote to local state:

object DiffEngine {
    fun diff(local: TerracottaProject, remote: TerracottaProject?): List<Operation>
}

docs.title: CLI Command Reference
docs.text: This reference describes the options and commands for the Terracotta CLI.

docs.title: Global Options
docs.text:
The following options apply to the root terracotta command and all subcommands:

  • -f, --file=<path>: Specifies the path to the configuration file (default: terracotta.yaml).
  • --modrinth-token=<token>: Modrinth API authentication token. If omitted, the CLI checks the MODRINTH_TOKEN environment variable.
  • -h, --help: Display help message and exit.
  • -V, --version: Display version information and exit.

docs.title: plan
docs.text:
Generate a semantic diff comparing your local terracotta.yaml config against the remote registry's project state. No changes are applied.

Usage: 

terracotta plan [options]

Output Indicators: - +: Indicates a resource (e.g. version) that will be uploaded or created. - -: Indicates a resource or metadata element (e.g. tags) that will be deleted or removed. - ~: Indicates metadata fields, tags, or descriptions that will be updated on the remote registry.

docs.title: apply
docs.text:
Applies the planned operations, updating registry metadata and uploading missing versions.

Usage: 

terracotta apply [options]

Requirements: - A valid MODRINTH_TOKEN environment variable or --modrinth-token option.

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: Terracotta configurations are written in YAML (usually named terracotta.yaml).

docs.title: Schema Specification
docs.text:
# Section defining project information
project:
  id: "my-plugin"           # Required. The project slug or ID in the registry
  name: "My Plugin"         # Required. Display title of the project
  summary: "Lightweight..." # Required. Short description / excerpt

description: "README.md"    # Required. Path to markdown file or raw text body

license: "MIT"              # Required. Project license identifier (e.g. MIT, Apache-2.0)

tags:                       # Optional. List of tags / categories
  - "paper"
  - "utility"

versions:                   # Optional. List of versions to verify and deploy
  - version: "1.2.0"        # Required. Unique version string identifier
    artifact: "libs/jar"    # Required. Path to the compiled binary file
    gameVersions:           # Required. List of supported Minecraft versions
      - "1.21.8"
      - "1.21.7"
    loaders:                # Optional/Required by registry. List of target platforms/mod loaders
      - "paper"
      - "spigot"

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This tutorial guides you through configuring and deploying a project to Modrinth using Terracotta.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. An account on Modrinth.
  2. A Modrinth API token. You can generate one in your Account Settings.
  3. Your compiled plugin artifact (e.g. build/libs/my-plugin-1.0.0.jar).
  4. Terracotta CLI binary installed.

docs.title: 1. Create your configuration
docs.text:
Create a file named terracotta.yaml in the root of your plugin project:

project:
  id: my-plugin
  name: My Plugin
  summary: Lightweight Paper plugin

description: README.md

versions:
  - version: 1.0.0
    artifact: build/libs/my-plugin-1.0.0.jar
    gameVersions:
      - 1.21.8
      - 1.21.7

tags:
  - paper
  - utility

license: MIT

Make sure that README.md exists and contains your project description.

docs.title: 2. Dry Run with plan
docs.text:
Define your Modrinth authentication token as an environment variable:

export MODRINTH_TOKEN="your_modrinth_api_token"

Run plan to check what operations Terracotta will execute:

terracotta plan

Terracotta will compare your terracotta.yaml configuration with the remote project on Modrinth and output the changes.

docs.title: 3. Apply the changes
docs.text:
When you are ready to upload, execute apply:

terracotta apply

Terracotta will now update the project metadata, set your tags, update the description from README.md, and upload your compiled JAR as version 1.0.0.

docs.title: Installing Terracotta
docs.text: This page describes how to download and install Terracotta CLI.

docs.title: GitHub Releases
docs.text: You can download compiled native binaries directly from the Releases page.

docs.title: Linux & macOS
docs.text:
1. Download the binary for your platform.
  2. Make it executable: 
    chmod +x terracotta
  3. Move it to your path: 
    mv terracotta /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
1. Download the terracotta-windows-amd64.exe binary.
  2. Add the directory containing the binary to your system PATH environment variable.

docs.title: SDKMAN! (Planned)
docs.text:
Once published, you will be able to install Terracotta via SDKMAN!:

sdk install terracotta
